**SGSC-AI Intelligence Probe Executed**

Xinli Electric Complete Equipment Group Co., Ltd. exemplifies strategic industrial relocation and a significant green energy pivot. Originally established in Shanghai, the group relocated its operations to Yueqing, Wenzhou—China's renowned hub for low-voltage electrical appliances—to capitalize on robust regional supply chains. Despite boasting a massive registered capital of 111.8 Million RMB and group-level designation, its active insured workforce consists of only 20 employees. This structural anomaly indicates an asset-light headquarters model focused on R&D, sales, bidding, and supply chain management rather than direct labor-intensive manufacturing. Furthermore, an analysis of its business scope reveals aggressive expansion beyond traditional power transmission equipment into New Energy Vehicle (NEV) infrastructure, including EV charging stations, centralized fast-charging networks, and photovoltaic components.
